topic: cairngorms fell running route — Ben Macdui

turns.content: thinking about doing ben macdui this weekend. as a run not a walk. been looking at the route from the ski centre car park at cairn gorm. is that the standard way up or is there a better option.

The Cairn Gorm ski centre car park is the most common starting point, yes. The standard route goes up past the weather station on Cairn Gorm summit, then follows the broad ridge south to Ben Macdui. It's about 16-17km round trip with around 900m of ascent.

There's also a route from Linn of Dee on the south side via Sron Riach, which is longer but arguably more scenic. For a run though, the ski centre route is probably better -- more runnable terrain on the plateau.

Are you planning to just do Ben Macdui as an out-and-back, or link it with Cairn Gorm summit too?
prob link both summits aye. if im going to be up there already seems daft not to. whats the terrain like on the plateau bit between the two. is it runnable or is it all boulders.

The plateau between Cairn Gorm and Ben Macdui is a mix. There are sections of good runnable ground -- firm, relatively smooth -- but also patches of boulder fields where you'll need to slow down and pick your way through. The worst of the boulders is around Lochan Buidhe in the middle section.

The path is reasonably well-worn so navigation isn't too bad in clear conditions, but the plateau is notoriously exposed. If the cloud comes in, it gets very featureless very quickly. Definitely want a GPS track loaded up even if the forecast looks good.

What's the weather looking like for the weekend?

saturday is meant to be clear in the morning with cloud coming in from the west around midday. so the plan would be early start, up and back before it closes in. prob leave the car park by half seven.

Half seven start sounds smart. If you're running it rather than walking, you could realistically be up Cairn Gorm in under an hour, across the plateau and up Macdui by maybe 9:30-10:00, and back to the car park by noon or earlier. That gives you a good buffer before the cloud rolls in.

February conditions up there though -- are you prepared for snow and ice on the upper sections? Cairn Gorm plateau in winter can be properly arctic.

aye ill take the micro spikes. theres definitely snow above about 900m right now. not planning on running through anything sketchy, if its icy on the steeper bits ill walk them. not worth a broken ankle.

Sensible approach. Micro spikes should handle most of what you'll encounter on the plateau. The steeper sections off Cairn Gorm summit heading south can be icy this time of year, so walking those is wise.

Kit-wise, are you carrying the usual winter essentials? Extra layer, bivvy bag, that sort of thing? The plateau is one of those places where conditions can turn fast.

aye full mountain kit. spare layer, waterproof, bivvy bag, headtorch, map and compass as well as the gps. emergency food. its the cairngorms in february not a park run. the wind chill up there can be minus twenty if it turns.

Exactly right. Good to hear you're treating it with the respect it deserves. Minus twenty wind chill is no joke -- people get caught out on that plateau every year.

One thing worth checking: the avalanche forecast for the Northern Cairngorms. The Sportscotland Avalanche Information Service usually updates daily. Probably not relevant if you're sticking to the plateau, but the steeper flanks can have wind slab building up.

good shout. ill check SAIS on friday night. not planning on going near any steep convex slopes but good to know what the snowpack is doing. last time I was up in the cairngorms in winter there was proper spindrift across the plateau. couldnt see ten metres. turned back.

Turning back in those conditions is always the right call, even if it's frustrating. The mountain will still be there next weekend. Sounds like you've got the experience and the judgement for this -- hope Saturday gives you a clear window. It's a brilliant run when the conditions cooperate.
aye fingers crossed. would be nice to actually see the view from the top for once instead of just standing in a cloud wondering why I do this to myself. right cheers for the route chat.
Ha, the classic Scottish summit experience -- effort for a view of grey. Hope you get lucky on Saturday. Enjoy the run.
